finding.summary: Excessive low-frequency energy relative to vocal midrange, likely causing a boomy, muddy tone.
finding.probable_cause: Proximity effect from close mic'ing or insufficient high-pass filtering; no EQ or filter present in FX chain to control low-end buildup.
finding.confidence_reason: Isolation is verified and silence fraction is very low (0.05), so the measured spectrum is reliable. The 100 Hz band is 6 dB louder than the 1 kHz band, which is atypical for a backing vocal stack and indicates excessive low-end content.

evidence_refs.description: Side RMS at -28 dBFS (6 dB lower than mid)
Empty FX chain – no high-pass filter or EQ available to address low end

proposal.reason: The track's excessive low-end energy requires a high-pass filter or corrective EQ, but no FX are present in the chain. The supported operations (set_track_volume, set_track_pan, set_fx_param, set_fx_bypass) cannot add a new plugin. A manual addition of a high-pass filter (~80–100 Hz) is recommended to reduce muddiness without compromising vocal clarity.
